title: Butter Chicken Classic Murgh Makhani

seo.title: Classic Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Master the art of authentic Indian Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) with our classic recipe. Creamy, rich, and flavorful – perfect for a homemade feast!
longTailKeywords: Butter Chicken Classic Murgh Makhani recipe
lowCompetitionKeywords: Indian Butter Chicken with tomato gravy
h2Headings: What is Classic Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani)?
Step-by-Step Guide to Making Butter Chicken
Tips for the Best Butter Chicken
seo.imageAlt: A bowl of classic Indian Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) garnished with fresh cilantro and cream, served with naan.
seo.imageCaption: Indulge in the rich, creamy flavors of our classic Butter Chicken, a beloved Indian dish perfect for any occasion.

description: Butter Chicken, or Murgh Makhani, is a world-renowned Indian dish celebrated for its rich, creamy tomato-based gravy and tender chicken pieces. This classic recipe delivers an unparalleled blend of smoky flavors from marinated chicken and a velvety texture, making it a true culinary delight. Perfect for special occasions or a comforting weeknight meal, it embodies the heart of Indian hospitality.

ingredients.item: Boneless, skinless chicken thighs or breast

ingredients.notes: soaked in warm water for 15 minutes if possible

instructions.title: Marinate the Chicken
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine the chicken pieces with yogurt, 1 tbsp ginger-garlic paste, lemon juice, red chili powder, turmeric powder, garam masala, and 1/2 tsp salt. Mix thoroughly to coat all chicken pieces. Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, or preferably 2-4 hours for best flavor.

instructions.title: Cook the Marinated Chicken
instructions.text: Heat 1 tbsp butter and 1 tbsp vegetable oil in a large heavy-bottomed pan or Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Add the marinated chicken in batches, ensuring not to overcrowd the pan. Cook until lightly browned and cooked through, about 6 minutes per batch. Remove cooked chicken and set aside.

instructions.title: Prepare the Gravy Base
instructions.text: In the same pan, add 1 tbsp butter. Once melted, add the finely chopped onion and sauté until translucent and lightly golden, about 6 minutes. Stir in the remaining 1 tbsp ginger-garlic paste, slit green chilies (if using), green cardamom pods, cinnamon stick, and bay leaf. Sauté for 2 minutes until fragrant.

instructions.title: Simmer the Tomato Gravy
instructions.text: Add the chopped tomatoes (or crushed tomatoes), soaked cashews, coriander powder, cumin powder, and the remaining 1/2 tsp salt. Mix well, bring the mixture to a gentle simmer, then cover the pan and cook for 10 minutes until the tomatoes are very soft and release their juices, forming a pulpy mixture.

instructions.title: Blend and Strain the Gravy
instructions.text: Remove the bay leaf, cinnamon stick, and cardamom pods (if easily found). Let the mixture cool slightly, then carefully transfer it to a blender. Blend until a completely smooth puree is formed. For an extra luxurious and velvety texture, pass the blended gravy through a fine-mesh sieve into a clean pot, pressing down on the solids to extract all liquid.

instructions.title: Combine and Simmer
instructions.text: Return the smooth gravy to the pot over medium heat. Stir in the cooked chicken pieces, the remaining 2 tbsp butter, granulated sugar, and the crushed fenugreek leaves (Kasuri Methi). Bring the curry to a gentle simmer and cook for 6 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld and the chicken to absorb the rich gravy.

instructions.title: Finish with Cream and Serve
instructions.text: Finally, stir in the heavy cream. Heat through gently for 2-3 minutes, ensuring the curry does not come to a rolling boil after adding the cream. Taste and adjust for salt and sugar if necessary. Garnish generously with fresh chopped cilantro. Serve hot with your favorite naan bread, roti, or fragrant basmati rice.

tips: For an authentic smoky flavor, you can grill or pan-fry the marinated chicken until slightly charred before adding it to the gravy.
If you want an extra rich gravy, toast the cashews lightly before soaking and blending them. You can also add a tablespoon of almond flour to thicken the gravy if needed.
Butter Chicken tastes even better the next day as the flavors deepen. Store leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.
article: Butter Chicken Classic Murgh Makhani is a beloved Indian Chicken Recipes dish enjoyed across Indian. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
